Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple An expert-level threat actor group known as 'FrostyGoop' targets water and wastewater systems using a straightforward yet effective approach. They exploit public-facing applications to gain initial access, execute malicious scripts, and disrupt system recovery processes.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-7247 (Suggested): smtp_mailaddr in smtp_session.c in OpenSMTPD, as used in OpenBSD and other products, allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands as root via a crafted SMTP session.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces Description: An adversary exploits a sample, demonstration, test, or debug interface that is unintentionally enabled on a production system, with the goal of gleaning information or leveraging functionality that would otherwise be unavailable.
stage.description: The attacker identifies and exploits a vulnerability in an internet-facing application, such as a web application or IoT device, allowing them to gain unauthorized access to the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36036 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver contains a privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160 Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs Description: Some APIs support scripting instructions as arguments. Methods that take scripted instructions (or references to scripted instructions) can be very flexible and powerful. However, if an attacker can specify the script that serves as input to
stage.description: Once inside the system, the attacker uses a command-line interface to execute malicious scripts, enabling further control over the compromised system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 607 Name: Obstruction Description: An attacker obstructs the interactions between system components. By interrupting or disabling these interactions, an adversary can often force the system into a degraded state or cause the system to stop working as intended. This can cause the system com
stage.description: To ensure their presence remains undetected and to hinder recovery efforts, the attacker disables critical system recovery options, leaving the system vulnerable and unable to quickly restore operations.

Scenario.summary: The FrostyGoop incident involves a sophisticated yet direct approach targeting water and wastewater systems. Initial access is gained through exploiting public-facing applications, followed by execution of malicious scripts to escalate privileges. Finally, the attackers inhibit system recovery, ensuring long-term disruption.
